market_regime.trend_or_range: Trending — S&P 500 hitting record highs per latest news; equity market in a broad uptrend supported by AI momentum and risk-on sentiment despite macro headwinds.
market_regime.mean_reversion_strategy: ACTIVE but selective — uptrend reduces the frequency of deep mean-reversion dips; prefer fading short-term overbought conditions on high-beta names rather than full contrarian reversals. Sector data is unavailable this cycle, so rely on individual ticker signals.
market_regime.vix_interpretation: VIX at 15.72 signals NORMAL / healthy market conditions. No systemic fear present in equities. This supports standard position sizing with no mandatory reduction. Volatility is low enough to consider modest size increases on high-conviction setups, but the macro backdrop (Hormuz, stagflation risk, Fed hawkish tilt) warrants keeping single-position exposure capped at 3–5% of book.
market_regime.position_sizing_guidance: Standard sizing permitted. Max single equity position: 5% of equity book. No VIX-driven haircut required at this level.

top_opportunities.rationale: AI boom narrative strongly reinforced by Snowflake's 37% jump on AI demand. NVDA is the primary beneficiary of enterprise AI infrastructure spend. With VIX low and momentum intact, any intraday pullback toward near-term support is a mean-reversion long entry. High-beta leader in the current BTC-led / AI-led risk-on regime.
top_opportunities.catalyst: Snowflake AI earnings blowout confirms accelerating AI capex cycle; NVDA is the picks-and-shovels play.
top_opportunities.position_size_adjustment: Standard (up to 5% of equity book). Set tight stop -4% below entry given elevated single-stock vol.

top_opportunities.rationale: S&P 500 at record highs implies NDX breadth is supportive. QQQ provides diversified tech exposure without single-stock earnings risk. Low VIX + AI tailwind = trend-following long is preferred strategy. Acts as a hedge if individual tech names disappoint.
top_opportunities.catalyst: Broad market record high; AI sector momentum; no earnings risk this week on the ETF itself.
top_opportunities.position_size_adjustment: Standard (up to 5% of equity book). Can scale to 6% given ETF diversification vs single stocks.

top_opportunities.rationale: Stagflation risk rising: Hormuz disruption threatens energy inflation, Fed's Musalem explicitly flagged a rate hike probability > 0 and higher neutral rate expectations. Gold is a direct beneficiary of both higher inflation expectations and fiscal/monetary uncertainty. Defensive allocation within a record-high equity environment.
top_opportunities.catalyst: Fed hawkish pivot risk, Hormuz/energy inflation, dollar uncertainty, bond market pricing higher neutral rate.
top_opportunities.position_size_adjustment: Reduce to 3–4% of equity book — acts as portfolio hedge, not primary alpha driver.

top_opportunities.rationale: Extreme Fear reading of 22 is historically a contrarian buy zone. BTC dominance at 57.6% confirms BTC is the primary flow absorber — capital is rotating INTO BTC, not out of crypto entirely. Total market cap ~$2.56T despite -1.51% 24h suggests consolidation, not collapse. Risk/reward for a mean-reversion long on BTC is favorable at these fear levels.
top_opportunities.catalyst: Extreme Fear contrarian signal; BTC dominance expanding (capital flight to quality within crypto); broader AI/crypto narrative from Trump administration policy tailwinds.
top_opportunities.position_size_adjustment: Reduce to 60% of normal crypto position size given Extreme Fear regime — size up only on confirmed bounce signal, not in anticipation.

top_opportunities.rationale: ETH at 9.53% dominance is near historically low levels relative to BTC, suggesting ETH is deeply oversold on a relative basis. In prior Extreme Fear recoveries, ETH tends to outperform once BTC stabilises. Mean-reversion setup: ETH is underperforming BTC (BTC-led regime) but this divergence typically closes on any sentiment recovery. Spot-only long with disciplined sizing.
top_opportunities.catalyst: Low relative dominance = mean-reversion candidate; ETH fundamentals (staking, L2 growth) intact; any sentiment recovery will benefit ETH disproportionately.
top_opportunities.position_size_adjustment: Reduce to 50% of normal crypto position size. ETH underperforms in BTC-led regimes — only enter on confirmed momentum shift or BTC stabilization.

top_opportunities.rationale: SOL has established itself as a high-beta but liquid alt with strong developer activity. In Extreme Fear environments that resolve to the upside, SOL historically delivers outsized recovery moves. Bybit liquidity on SOL/USDT is deep enough for bot execution. Wait for BTC to stabilise before entry.
top_opportunities.catalyst: High-beta recovery play; strong on-chain metrics; Bybit liquidity adequate. Contingent on BTC floor formation.
top_opportunities.position_size_adjustment: Limit to 30–40% of normal crypto position size. High-beta alt in a risk-off crypto regime — only activate if BTC mean-reversion long is triggered first.

ADD.reason: Snowflake jumped 37% on AI earnings beat — potential mean-reversion fade or momentum continuation setup depending on post-gap behavior. Add to monitor for pullback entry.
High liquidity on Bybit, strong mean-reversion characteristics, and BNB tends to hold relative value in BTC-led regimes due to exchange utility demand.

REMOVE.reason: DOT has declining developer activity and low relative volume. In a BTC-led Extreme Fear regime, DOT underperforms with limited recovery upside. Poor mean-reversion profile currently.
ADA has weak relative strength in BTC-led regimes and thin institutional sponsorship. Low probability of mean-reversion outperformance in current environment.

AVOID.reason: Low liquidity relative to other watchlist pairs on Bybit; wide spreads in Extreme Fear regimes create slippage risk for the bot. Avoid until regime improves.

best_mean_reversion_sectors.rationale: AI momentum driving record highs; any intraday pullbacks in tech names are buyable dips. VIX is low enough to sustain trend. NVDA and MSFT are primary vehicles.
Stagflation environment (Hormuz/energy inflation + slower growth) historically benefits materials. GLD and SLV on equity watchlist align here. Mean-reversion longs on any dip warranted.
META is on the equity watchlist and XLC benefits from AI-driven ad revenue growth. Sector likely participating in the record-high rally. Watch for pullback entries.

avoid_or_underweight.rationale: Hormuz disruption creates binary risk — energy prices could spike OR normalise suddenly. While inflation benefits XLE, geopolitical uncertainty creates unpredictable volatility. Avoid directional bets; let USO act as the hedge vehicle if needed.
Higher neutral rate expectations flagged by Fed's Musalem are directly bearish for rate-sensitive utilities. Rising long-end yields compress utility valuations.
Same rate logic as utilities — Fed hawkish tilt (rate hike probability > 0, higher neutral rate) is structurally negative for REITs. Underweight until rate path clarifies.

crypto_outlook.sentiment_read: Fear & Greed Index at 22 (Extreme Fear) with a further -3 deterioration in 24 hours signals maximum pessimism, which historically precedes mean-reversion recoveries. The bot should treat current levels as a contrarian opportunity zone, but size conservatively and wait for confirmed stabilisation before full deployment.
crypto_outlook.dominance_read: BTC dominance at 57.6% is elevated and rising, confirming capital is consolidating into Bitcoin as the perceived safe haven within crypto. Altcoins are underperforming and will continue to lag until BTC establishes a clear floor — ETH at 9.53% dominance suggests it is near a relative value floor vs BTC.

top_crypto_opportunities.rationale: Primary mean-reversion candidate. Extreme Fear + BTC dominance absorption = highest probability recovery vehicle. Enter on stabilisation signal, target 5–8% recovery. Stop -6% below entry.
Deeply oversold on relative basis vs BTC. Mean-reversion long with the expectation that ETH/BTC ratio recovers once sentiment stabilises. Only activate after BTC long is triggered.
High-beta recovery play for when sentiment inflects. Smallest position size, last to activate. Strong developer ecosystem provides fundamental support for recovery.

crypto_risk_warnings.detail: Fear & Greed at 22 and declining (-3 in 24h). If this continues toward single digits, do not deploy remaining dry powder. Hold cash and wait for a reversal signal. Capitulation can extend further than models suggest.
Fed's Musalem explicitly stated a rate hike probability > 0. A surprise hawkish statement or economic data print could accelerate crypto sell-off. BTC and risk assets would reprice lower immediately.
Hormuz disruption is introducing stagflation risk. A major escalation (closure of the strait) would cause a risk-off cascade across all asset classes including crypto. Monitor energy news closely.
Total market cap at $2.56T with -1.51% 24h change. A break below key psychological support levels ($2.4T) would signal a broader deleveraging event — pause all new crypto entries if this occurs.

Trump crypto policy narrative is active per news cycle. Policy reversals, SEC actions, or Congressional developments could create sudden volatility. The positive narrative could also reverse — monitor for regulatory headlines.
In BTC-led Extreme Fear regimes, altcoin liquidity dries up rapidly. SOL, LINK, NEAR, DOT, and ADA may have significantly wider spreads than normal — verify spread and depth before any alt execution.

macro_events.detail: Fed's Musalem has explicitly raised the possibility of a rate hike (> 0 probability). Any further hawkish Fed speakers or surprise economic data (CPI, PCE, jobs) this week could trigger a sharp equity re-rating and crypto sell-off. Monitor Fed calendar closely.

Hormuz disruption is described as 'silently choking the US economy.' Energy supply shock + slower growth = stagflation scenario. This is negative for growth equities (tech), positive for gold/materials, and broadly negative for crypto in the short term.

macro_events.event: Bond Market Volatility — Higher Neutral Rate Pricing
macro_events.detail: Bond markets pricing a higher neutral rate per Musalem. Rising long-end yields compress equity P/E multiples, particularly for high-growth tech. TLT on the watchlist is a key barometer — watch for continued TLT weakness as a warning signal for equity valuations.

S&P 500 at record highs with VIX at 15.72 suggests positioning may be stretched. A single macro shock (Fed, Hormuz, geopolitical) could cause rapid de-risking. Avoid chasing momentum without confirmed mean-reversion signals.

Canada PM Carney statements indicate active trade renegotiation with the US. Unexpected tariff escalation or trade deal collapse could affect CAD-denominated assets and US equities with Canada exposure.

specific_risk_flags: TLT is a canary — if TLT breaks down further, reduce all equity longs, especially rate-sensitive sectors.
Crypto Extreme Fear (22) means crypto positions should be sized at 50–60% of normal until sentiment recovers above 30.
NVDA post-Snowflake rally may be extended — wait for a 3–5% intraday pullback before initiating, do not chase the open.
No sector performance data available this cycle — sector rotation analysis is based on macro inference only. Do not deploy sector-specific ETF strategies until data is confirmed.
Earnings list is empty — verify with Alpaca/external source before trading individual names mid-week, as unexpected earnings can cause gap risk.

max_position_size.crypto_btc: 60% of normal crypto position size (Extreme Fear regime)
max_position_size.overall_crypto_book_deployment: Do not exceed 70% of total crypto capital until Fear & Greed recovers above 30

confidence_reason: Equities present a reasonably constructive environment (VIX 15.72, S&P at record highs, AI momentum) supporting a score of 6–7 on their own, but crypto conditions (Extreme Fear at 22, BTC-led risk-off, total market cap declining) drag the blended score down significantly — combined with a real Fed rate hike risk, Hormuz stagflation headwind, and missing sector/crypto scanner data that reduces signal quality, a score of 5 reflects cautious engagement with reduced position sizes across both books.
